title: HUD, Text, Menus, Focus, Multichannel Cues, and Motion Settings · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A mixed-input public game-night team. They need reconciled score and waves, safe respawn, descriptive difficulty and assistance options, explicit sprite ownership, bounded audio channels, separate volume and mute, scalable text, visible focus, multichannel cues, pause, and reduced effects. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Progression, Audio, and Interface with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.
Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing.
Understand kill as group removal, release external indexes and resources, and reconcile created, active, and retired counts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Score Ledger
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Asteroids team handles case 1303 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Score Ledger.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Audit the initial evidence packet · Project Motion Photosensitivity
steps.instruction:

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-motion-photosensitivity-1pnvkt
# Competency: asteroids-motion-photosensitivity.
# Case 9815: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_motion_photosensitivity_1pnvkt(critical_signals: int = 8, multi_channel_signals: int = 8, focusable_controls: int = 7, focused_controls: int = 7, reduced_motion: bool = True, scalable_text: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1pnvkt = "1pnvkt-tkvnp1-9815"
    assert evidence_variant_1pnvkt
    evidence_axes_1pnvkt = (
        "player1pnvkt",
        "seed9815",
        "inputtkvnp1",
        "frame1pnvkt9815",
        "state98151pnvkt",
        "playtesttkvnp19815",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1pnvkt)) == 6
    if critical_signals < 1 or focusable_controls < 1:
        return False, critical_signals, "ui-domain"
    if multi_channel_signals != critical_signals:
        return False, multi_channel_signals, "color-only-signal"
    if focused_controls != focusable_controls:
        return False, focused_controls, "focus-gap"
    if not reduced_motion or not scalable_text:
        return False, focusable_controls, "display-access"
    assert focused_controls == focusable_controls
    return True, focused_controls, "accessible-game-ui"
